metadata.title: Ukraine Electric Power Grid Attack Scenario

Scenario.overview: Power Grid Systems Complex The attackers aim to disrupt the Ukraine electric power grid by exploiting various vulnerabilities and gaining unauthorized access to critical systems.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets Cloud Instance Target Artifact (Tasks) File System User Account Credential Store Network Traffic Application Log Process
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Delivery Method Process Creation (Mechanism) Artificial Intelligence Code Injection Command Execution (Mechanism) Application Log Content (Mechanism) OS API Execution (Mechanism) User Account Metadata (Mechanism) Reg.exe Utility Network Traffic Flow (Mechanism) File Access (Mechanism) Network Traffic Content (Mechanism) File Modification (Mechanism)

stage.name: Reconnaissance
stage.description: The attackers gather information about Ukrainian power grid systems using artificial intelligence.

stage.name: Resource Development
stage.capec: CAPEC-670: Software Development Tools Maliciously Altered
stage.description: The attackers create custom malware to evade detection in cloud instances.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.kev: CVE-2019-17026 (Suggested): Mozilla Firefox and Thunderbird contain a type confusion vulnerability due to incorrect alias information in the IonMonkey JIT compiler when setting array elements.
stage.description: The attackers use a compromised website to infect systems with BlackEnergy3 via drive-by download.

stage.name: Execution
stage.kev: CVE-2021-26084 (Suggested): Atlassian Confluence Server and Data Server contain an Object-Graph Navigation Language (OGNL) injection vulnerability that may allow an unauthenticated attacker to execute code.
stage.description: The attackers inject malicious code into infected systems.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.capec: CAPEC-516: Hardware Component Substitution During Baselining
stage.description: The attackers install a rootkit to maintain persistence.

stage.name: Privilege Escalation
stage.description: The attackers gain elevated privileges on infected systems.

stage.name: Defense Evasion
stage.description: The attackers disable antivirus software to avoid detection.

stage.name: Credential Access
stage.description: The attackers discover weak passwords for lateral movement.

stage.name: Discovery
stage.description: The attackers scan networks for vulnerable systems.

stage.name: Lateral Movement
stage.description: The attackers move laterally through remote services.

stage.name: Collection
stage.description: The attackers collect data from infected systems.

stage.name: Exfiltration
stage.description: The attackers exfiltrate collected data using FTP.

stage.name: Impact
stage.capec: CAPEC-681: Exploitation of Improperly Controlled Hardware Security Identifiers
stage.description: The attackers disrupt power grid operations.

Scenario.summary: The attackers employ a complex attack chain involving reconnaissance, resource development, initial access, execution, persistence, privilege escalation, defense evasion, credential access, discovery, lateral movement, collection, exfiltration, and impact to disrupt the Ukraine electric power grid.
